                [32m'## Requirements\n'[39m +

                [32m'## Implementation Guide\n'[39m +
                [32m'1. Initialize a Node.js project in `/home/user/inconvo-app`.\n'[39m +

                [32m'3. Create `index.js`:\n'[39m +

                [32m'   - Initialize the Inconvo client using `INCONVO_API_KEY` and `INCONVO_AGENT_ID`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'   - Call `client.conversations.create({ context: { user_id: 123 } })` to start a conversation.\n'[39m +
                [32m'   - Call `client.conversations.messages.create(id, { message: "What are my top products?" })`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'   - Save the returned response to `response.json` using `fs.writeFileSync`.\n'[39m +
                [32m'4. Run the script to generate `response.json`.\n'[39m +

                    [32m'const log_1 = require("./internal/utils/log.js");\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const values_2 = require("./internal/utils/values.js");\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * API Client for interfacing with the Inconvo API.\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class Inconvo {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * API Client for interfacing with the Inconvo API.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m"     * @param {string | undefined} [opts.apiKey=process.env['INCONVO_API_KEY'] ?? undefined]\n"[39m +
                    [32m"     * @param {string} [opts.baseURL=process.env['INCONVO_BASE_URL'] ?? https://app.inconvo.ai/api/v1] - Override the default base URL for the API.\n"[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {number} [opts.timeout=1 minute] - The maximum amount of time (in milliseconds) the client will wait for a response before timing out.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {MergedRequestInit} [opts.fetchOptions] - Additional `RequestInit` options to be passed to `fetch` calls.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {Fetch} [opts.fetch] - Specify a custom `fetch` function implementation.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {number} [opts.maxRetries=2] - The maximum number of times the client will retry a request.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {HeadersLike} opts.defaultHeaders - Default headers to include with every request to the API.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {Record<string, string | undefined>} opts.defaultQuery - Default query parameters to include with every request to the API.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m"    constructor({ baseURL = (0, env_1.readEnv)('INCONVO_BASE_URL'), apiKey = (0, env_1.readEnv)('INCONVO_API_KEY'), ...opts } = {}) {\n"[39m +
                    [32m'        _Inconvo_instances.add(this);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        _Inconvo_encoder.set(this, void 0);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.agents = new API.Agents(this);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if (apiKey === undefined) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m`            throw new Errors.InconvoError("The INCONVO_API_KEY environment variable is missing or empty; either provide it, or instantiate the Inconvo client with an apiKey option, like new Inconvo({ apiKey: 'My API Key' }).");\n`[39m +
                    [32m'        }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const options = {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            apiKey,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            ...opts,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            baseURL: baseURL || `https://app.inconvo.ai/api/v1`,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        };\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.baseURL = options.baseURL;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.timeout = options.timeout ?? _a.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT /* 1 minute */;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.logger = options.logger ?? console;\n'[39m +
                    [32m"        const defaultLogLevel = 'warn';\n"[39m +
                    [32m'        // Set default logLevel early so that we can log a warning in parseLogLevel.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.logLevel = defaultLogLevel;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.logLevel =\n'[39m +
                    [32m"            (0, log_1.parseLogLevel)(options.logLevel, 'ClientOptions.logLevel', this) ??\n"[39m +
                    [32m`                (0, log_1.parseLogLevel)((0, env_1.readEnv)('INCONVO_LOG'), "process.env['INCONVO_LOG']", this) ??\n`[39m +
                    [32m'                defaultLogLevel;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.fetchOptions = options.fetchOptions;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.maxRetries = options.maxRetries ?? 2;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.fetch = options.fetch ?? Shims.getDefaultFetch();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        tslib_1.__classPrivateFieldSet(this, _Inconvo_encoder, Opts.FallbackEncoder, "f");\n'[39m +
                    [32m"        const customHeadersEnv = (0, env_1.readEnv)('INCONVO_CUSTOM_HEADERS');\n"[39m +
                    [32m'        if (customHeadersEnv) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            const parsed = {};\n'[39m +
                    [32m"            for (const line of customHeadersEnv.split('\\n')) {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"                const colon = line.indexOf(':');\n"[39m +
                    [32m'                if (colon >= 0) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                    parsed[line.substring(0, colon).trim()] = line.substring(colon + 1).trim();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            options.defaultHeaders = { ...parsed, ...options.defaultHeaders };\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this._options = options;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.apiKey = apiKey;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Create a new client instance re-using the same options given to the current client with optional overriding.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    withOptions(options) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const client = new this.constructor({\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            ...this._options,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            baseURL: this.baseURL,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            maxRetries: this.maxRetries,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            timeout: this.timeout,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            logger: this.logger,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            logLevel: this.logLevel,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            fetch: this.fetch,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            fetchOptions: this.fetchOptions,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            apiKey: this.apiKey,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            ...options,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        });\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return client;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    defaultQuery() {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return this._options.defaultQuery;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    validateHeaders({ values, nulls }) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async authHeaders(opts) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return (0, headers_1.buildHeaders)([{ Authorization: `Bearer ${this.apiKey}` }]);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    stringifyQuery(query) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return (0, query_1.stringifyQuery)(query);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    getUserAgent() {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return `${this.constructor.name}/JS ${version_1.VERSION}`;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    defaultIdempotencyKey() {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return `stainless-node-retry-${(0, uuid_1.uuid4)()}`;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    makeStatusError(status, error, message, headers) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return Errors.APIError.generate(status, error, message, headers);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    buildURL(path, query, defaultBaseURL) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const baseURL = (!tslib_1.__classPrivateFieldGet(this, _Inconvo_instances, "m", _Inconvo_baseURLOverridden).call(this) && defaultBaseURL) || this.baseURL;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const url = (0, values_1.isAbsoluteURL)(path) ?\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            new URL(path)\n'[39m +
                    [32m"            : new URL(baseURL + (baseURL.endsWith('/') && path.startsWith('/') ? path.slice(1) : path));\n"[39m +
                    [32m'        const defaultQuery = this.defaultQuery();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const pathQuery = Object.fromEntries(url.searchParams);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if (!(0, values_2.isEmptyObj)(defaultQuery) || !(0, values_2.isEmptyObj)(pathQuery)) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            query = { ...pathQuery, ...defaultQuery, ...query };\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        }\n'[39m +
                    [32m"        if (typeof query === 'object' && query && !Array.isArray(query)) {\n"[39m +
                    [32m'            url.search = this.stringifyQuery(query);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return url.toString();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Used as a callback for mutating the given `FinalRequestOptions` object.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async prepareOptions(options) { }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Used as a callback for mutating the given `RequestInit` object.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * This is useful for cases where you want to add certain headers based off of\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * the request properties, e.g. `method` or `url`.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async prepareRequest(request, { url, options }) { }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    get(path, opts) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m"        return this.methodRequest('get', path, opts);\n"[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    post(path, opts) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m"        return this.methodRequest('post', path, opts);\n"[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    patch(path, opts) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m"        return this.methodRequest('patch', path, opts);\n"[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    put(path, opts) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m"        return this.methodRequest('put', path, opts);\n"[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    delete(path, opts) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m"        return this.methodRequest('delete', path, opts);\n"[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    methodRequest(method, path, opts) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return this.request(Promise.resolve(opts).then((opts) => {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            return { method, path, ...opts };\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        }));\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    request(options, remainingRetries = null) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return new api_promise_1.APIPromise(this, this.makeRequest(options, remainingRetries, undefined));\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    async makeRequest(optionsInput, retriesRemaining, retryOfRequestLogID) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const options = await optionsInput;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const maxRetries = options.maxRetries ?? this.maxRetries;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if (retriesRemaining == null) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            retriesRemaining = maxRetries;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        await this.prepareOptions(options);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const { req, url, timeout } = await this.buildRequest(options, {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            retryCount: maxRetries - retriesRemaining,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        });\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        await this.prepareRequest(req, { url, options });\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        /** Not an API request ID, just for correlating local log entries. */\n'[39m +
                    [32m"        const requestLogID = 'log_' + ((Math.random() * (1 << 24)) | 0).toString(16).padStart(6, '0');\n"[39m +
                    [32m"        const retryLogStr = retryOfRequestLogID === undefined ? '' : `, retryOf: ${retryOfRequestLogID}`;\n"[39m +
                    [32m'        const startTime = Date.now();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        (0, log_1.loggerFor)(this).debug(`[${requestLogID}] sending request`, (0, log_1.formatRequestDetails)({\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            retryOfRequestLogID,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            method: options.method,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            url,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            options,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            headers: req.headers,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        }));\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if (options.signal?.aborted) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            throw new Errors.APIUserAbortError();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const controller = new AbortController();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const response = await this.fetchWithTimeout(url, req, timeout, controller).catch(errors_1.castToError);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const headersTime = Date.now();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if (response instanceof globalThis.Error) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            const retryMessage = `retrying, ${retriesRemaining} attempts remaining`;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            if (options.signal?.aborted) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                throw new Errors.APIUserAbortError();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            // detect native connection timeout errors\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            // deno throws "TypeError: error sending request for url (https://example/): client error (Connect): tcp connect error: Operation timed out (os error 60): Operation timed out (os error 60)"\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            // undici throws "TypeError: fetch failed" with cause "ConnectTimeoutError: Connect Timeout Error (attempted address: example:443, timeout: 1ms)'[39m... 13975 more characters,
                  isTruncated: [33mfalse[39m

                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Retrieve conversation\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @example\n'[39m +

                    [32m'     * const inconvoConversation =\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *   await client.agents.conversations.retrieve(\n'[39m +
                    [32m"     *     'conversation_id',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"     *     { agentId: 'agentId' },\n"[39m +
                    [32m'     *   );\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * ```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    retrieve(conversationID, params, options) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const { agentId } = params;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return this._client.get((0, path_1.path) `/agents/${agentId}/conversations/${conversationID}`, options);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * List conversations\n'[39m +

                    [32m'const sse_stream_1 = require("../../../../core/sse-stream.js");\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\n'[39m +
                    [32m" * Send a user message and receive Inconvo's structured reply (synchronous or SSE stream)\n"[39m +
                    [32m' */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'class Response extends resource_1.APIResource {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    constructor() {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        super(...arguments);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        this.feedback = new FeedbackAPI.FeedbackResource(this._client);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    create(id, params, options) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const { agentId, ...body } = params;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        if (body.stream === true) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            return this._createStreaming(id, agentId, body, options);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return this._client.post((0, path_1.path) `/agents/${agentId}/conversations/${id}/response`, { body, ...options });\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Get a response\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @example\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * ```ts\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * const response =\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *   await client.agents.conversations.response.retrieve(\n'[39m +
                    [32m"     *     'response_id',\n"[39m +
                    [32m'     *     {\n'[39m +
                    [32m"     *       agentId: 'agentId',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"     *       conversation_id: 'conversation_id',\n"[39m +
                    [32m'     *     },\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *   );\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * ```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    retrieve(responseID, params, options) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const { agentId, conversation_id } = params;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return this._client.get((0, path_1.path) `/agents/${agentId}/conversations/${conversation_id}/response/${responseID}`, options);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    _createStreaming(id, agentId, body, options) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        // Create a controller for aborting the request if needed\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const controller = globalThis.AbortController ? new globalThis.AbortController() : undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const requestOptions = {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            ...options,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            headers: {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'                ...options?.headers,\n'[39m +
                    [32m"                Accept: 'text/event-stream',\n"[39m +
                    [32m'            },\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            signal: controller?.signal,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            __binaryResponse: true,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        };\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        // When __binaryResponse is true, post() returns the raw Response directly\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        const responsePromise = this._client.post((0, path_1.path) `/agents/${agentId}/conversations/${id}/response`, {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            body,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'            ...requestOptions,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        });\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        return (0, sse_stream_1.createSSEStream)(responsePromise, controller);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'}\n'[39m +
                    [32m'exports.Response = Response;\n'[39m +

              toolName: [32m'readFile'[39m,

                type: [32m'json'[39m,
                value: {
                  content: [32m'export { Inconvo as default } from "./client.js";\n'[39m +

                    [32m'//# sourceMappingURL=index.d.ts.map'[39m,
                  isTruncated: [33mfalse[39m
                }

              providerOptions: {
                vertex: {
                  thoughtSignature: [32m''[39m

        {
          role: [32m'tool'[39m,
          content: [
            {
              type: [32m'tool-result'[39m,
              toolCallId: [32m'hXbVUcltgZjlHxhs'[39m,
              toolName: [32m'readFile'[39m,
              output: {
                type: [32m'json'[39m,
                value: {
                  content: [32m'import type { RequestInit, RequestInfo } from "./internal/builtin-types.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import type { PromiseOrValue, MergedRequestInit, FinalizedRequestInit } from "./internal/types.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export type { Logger, LogLevel } from "./internal/utils/log.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import * as Opts from "./internal/request-options.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import * as Errors from "./core/error.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import * as Pagination from "./core/pagination.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { type ConversationsCursorParams, ConversationsCursorResponse } from "./core/pagination.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import * as Uploads from "./core/uploads.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import * as API from "./resources/index.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { APIPromise } from "./core/api-promise.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { Agents } from "./resources/agents/agents.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { type Fetch } from "./internal/builtin-types.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { HeadersLike, NullableHeaders } from "./internal/headers.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { FinalRequestOptions, RequestOptions } from "./internal/request-options.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'import { type LogLevel, type Logger } from "./internal/utils/log.js";\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export interface ClientOptions {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m"     * Defaults to process.env['INCONVO_API_KEY'].\n"[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    apiKey?: string | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Override the default base URL for the API, e.g., "https://api.example.com/v2/"\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m"     * Defaults to process.env['INCONVO_BASE_URL'].\n"[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    baseURL?: string | null | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * The maximum amount of time (in milliseconds) that the client should wait for a response\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * from the server before timing out a single request.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Note that request timeouts are retried by default, so in a worst-case scenario you may wait\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * much longer than this timeout before the promise succeeds or fails.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @unit milliseconds\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    timeout?: number | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Additional `RequestInit` options to be passed to `fetch` calls.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Properties will be overridden by per-request `fetchOptions`.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    fetchOptions?: MergedRequestInit | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Specify a custom `fetch` function implementation.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * If not provided, we expect that `fetch` is defined globally.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    fetch?: Fetch | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * The maximum number of times that the client will retry a request in case of a\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * temporary failure, like a network error or a 5XX error from the server.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @default 2\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    maxRetries?: number | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Default headers to include with every request to the API.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * These can be removed in individual requests by explicitly setting the\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * header to `null` in request options.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    defaultHeaders?: HeadersLike | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Default query parameters to include with every request to the API.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * These can be removed in individual requests by explicitly setting the\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * param to `undefined` in request options.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    defaultQuery?: Record<string, string | undefined> | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Set the log level.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m"     * Defaults to process.env['INCONVO_LOG'] or 'warn' if it isn't set.\n"[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    logLevel?: LogLevel | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Set the logger.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Defaults to globalThis.console.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    logger?: Logger | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'}\n'[39m +
                    [32m'/**\n'[39m +
                    [32m' * API Client for interfacing with the Inconvo API.\n'[39m +
                    [32m' */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare class Inconvo {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    #private;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    apiKey: string;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    baseURL: string;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    maxRetries: number;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    timeout: number;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    logger: Logger;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    logLevel: LogLevel | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    fetchOptions: MergedRequestInit | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private fetch;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    protected idempotencyHeader?: string;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private _options;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * API Client for interfacing with the Inconvo API.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m"     * @param {string | undefined} [opts.apiKey=process.env['INCONVO_API_KEY'] ?? undefined]\n"[39m +
                    [32m"     * @param {string} [opts.baseURL=process.env['INCONVO_BASE_URL'] ?? https://app.inconvo.ai/api/v1] - Override the default base URL for the API.\n"[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {number} [opts.timeout=1 minute] - The maximum amount of time (in milliseconds) the client will wait for a response before timing out.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {MergedRequestInit} [opts.fetchOptions] - Additional `RequestInit` options to be passed to `fetch` calls.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {Fetch} [opts.fetch] - Specify a custom `fetch` function implementation.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {number} [opts.maxRetries=2] - The maximum number of times the client will retry a request.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {HeadersLike} opts.defaultHeaders - Default headers to include with every request to the API.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * @param {Record<string, string | undefined>} opts.defaultQuery - Default query parameters to include with every request to the API.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    constructor({ baseURL, apiKey, ...opts }?: ClientOptions);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Create a new client instance re-using the same options given to the current client with optional overriding.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    withOptions(options: Partial<ClientOptions>): this;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    protected defaultQuery(): Record<string, string | undefined> | undefined;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    protected validateHeaders({ values, nulls }: NullableHeaders): void;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    protected authHeaders(opts: FinalRequestOptions): Promise<NullableHeaders | undefined>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    protected stringifyQuery(query: object | Record<string, unknown>): string;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private getUserAgent;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    protected defaultIdempotencyKey(): string;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    protected makeStatusError(status: number, error: Object, message: string | undefined, headers: Headers): Errors.APIError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    buildURL(path: string, query: Record<string, unknown> | null | undefined, defaultBaseURL?: string | undefined): string;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Used as a callback for mutating the given `FinalRequestOptions` object.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    protected prepareOptions(options: FinalRequestOptions): Promise<void>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    /**\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * Used as a callback for mutating the given `RequestInit` object.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     *\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * This is useful for cases where you want to add certain headers based off of\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     * the request properties, e.g. `method` or `url`.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'     */\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    protected prepareRequest(request: RequestInit, { url, options }: {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        url: string;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        options: FinalRequestOptions;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }): Promise<void>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    get<Rsp>(path: string, opts?: PromiseOrValue<RequestOptions>): APIPromise<Rsp>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    post<Rsp>(path: string, opts?: PromiseOrValue<RequestOptions>): APIPromise<Rsp>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    patch<Rsp>(path: string, opts?: PromiseOrValue<RequestOptions>): APIPromise<Rsp>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    put<Rsp>(path: string, opts?: PromiseOrValue<RequestOptions>): APIPromise<Rsp>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    delete<Rsp>(path: string, opts?: PromiseOrValue<RequestOptions>): APIPromise<Rsp>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private methodRequest;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    request<Rsp>(options: PromiseOrValue<FinalRequestOptions>, remainingRetries?: number | null): APIPromise<Rsp>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private makeRequest;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    getAPIList<Item, PageClass extends Pagination.AbstractPage<Item> = Pagination.AbstractPage<Item>>(path: string, Page: new (...args: any[]) => PageClass, opts?: PromiseOrValue<RequestOptions>): Pagination.PagePromise<PageClass, Item>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    requestAPIList<Item = unknown, PageClass extends Pagination.AbstractPage<Item> = Pagination.AbstractPage<Item>>(Page: new (...args: ConstructorParameters<typeof Pagination.AbstractPage>) => PageClass, options: PromiseOrValue<FinalRequestOptions>): Pagination.PagePromise<PageClass, Item>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    fetchWithTimeout(url: RequestInfo, init: RequestInit | undefined, ms: number, controller: AbortController): Promise<Response>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private shouldRetry;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private retryRequest;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private calculateDefaultRetryTimeoutMillis;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    buildRequest(inputOptions: FinalRequestOptions, { retryCount }?: {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        retryCount?: number;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }): Promise<{\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        req: FinalizedRequestInit;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        url: string;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'        timeout: number;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }>;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private buildHeaders;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private _makeAbort;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    private buildBody;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static Inconvo: typeof Inconvo;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static DEFAULT_TIMEOUT: number;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static InconvoError: typeof Errors.InconvoError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static APIError: typeof Errors.APIError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static APIConnectionError: typeof Errors.APIConnectionError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static APIConnectionTimeoutError: typeof Errors.APIConnectionTimeoutError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static APIUserAbortError: typeof Errors.APIUserAbortError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static NotFoundError: typeof Errors.NotFoundError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static ConflictError: typeof Errors.ConflictError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static RateLimitError: typeof Errors.RateLimitError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static BadRequestError: typeof Errors.BadRequestError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static AuthenticationError: typeof Errors.AuthenticationError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static InternalServerError: typeof Errors.InternalServerError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static PermissionDeniedError: typeof Errors.PermissionDeniedError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static UnprocessableEntityError: typeof Errors.UnprocessableEntityError;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    static toFile: typeof Uploads.toFile;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    agents: API.Agents;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'}\n'[39m +
                    [32m'export declare namespace Inconvo {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    export type RequestOptions = Opts.RequestOptions;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    export import ConversationsCursor = Pagination.ConversationsCursor;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    export { type ConversationsCursorParams as ConversationsCursorParams, type ConversationsCursorResponse as ConversationsCursorResponse, };\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    export { Agents as Agents };\n'[39m +
                    [32m'}\n'[39m +
                    [32m'//# sourceMappingURL=client.d.ts.map'[39m,
                  isTruncated: [33mfalse[39m
                }
              },
              providerOptions: {
                vertex: {
                  thoughtSignature: [32m'AY89a1/v814xwBj9cwZJRS4/DfhM11/oPwjZ8y3Nucu9CFp9LMcBjXdsos8cXDKzpD/s2fwQP/nd41uEI1l58ve6XrJKZaxppaE0/+8fAsc6gfaouQ=='[39m

                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m'**Clarifying Conversation Creation**\n'[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m"I'm examining the `conversations.js` file and it appears that the `create` method on conversations requires an `agentID`. This contradicts the user's example of `client.conversations.create(body)`. I'm considering if the user intends for me to wrap the client in a way that abstracts this requirement.\n"[39m +
                [32m'\n'[39m +
                [32m''[39m
            },
            {
              type: [32m'tool-call'[39m,

            {
              type: [32m'tool-result'[39m,

              toolName: [32m'readFile'[39m,
              output: {
                type: [32m'json'[39m,
                value: {
                  content: [32m'# Inconvo TypeScript API Library\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'[![NPM version](<https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@inconvoai/node.svg?label=npm%20(stable)>)](https://npmjs.org/package/@inconvoai/node) ![npm bundle size](https://img.shields.io/bundlephobia/minzip/@inconvoai/node)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'This library provides convenient access to the Inconvo REST API from server-side TypeScript or JavaScript.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'The REST API documentation can be found on [inconvo.com](https://inconvo.com/docs). The full API of this library can be found in [api.md](api.md).\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'It is generated with [Stainless](https://www.stainless.com/).\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'## Installation\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```sh\n'[39m +
                    [32m'npm install @inconvoai/node\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'## Usage\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'The full API of this library can be found in [api.md](api.md).\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'<!-- prettier-ignore -->\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```js\n'[39m +
                    [32m"import Inconvo from '@inconvoai/node';\n"[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const client = new Inconvo({\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  apiKey: process.env['INCONVO_API_KEY'], // This is the default and can be omitted\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m"const conversation = await client.agents.conversations.create('agentId', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  userIdentifier: 'user_123',\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'console.log(conversation.id);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'### Request & Response types\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'This library includes TypeScript definitions for all request params and response fields. You may import and use them like so:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'<!-- prettier-ignore -->\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```ts\n'[39m +
                    [32m"import Inconvo from '@inconvoai/node';\n"[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const client = new Inconvo({\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  apiKey: process.env['INCONVO_API_KEY'], // This is the default and can be omitted\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const params: Inconvo.Agents.ConversationCreateParams = {\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  userIdentifier: 'user_123',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  userContext: { orgId: 'org_456' },\n"[39m +
                    [32m'};\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const conversation: Inconvo.Agents.ConversationCreateResponse =\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  await client.agents.conversations.create('agentId', params);\n"[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Documentation for each method, request param, and response field are available in docstrings and will appear on hover in most modern editors.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'## File uploads\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Request parameters that correspond to file uploads can be passed in many different forms:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'- `File` (or an object with the same structure)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'- a `fetch` `Response` (or an object with the same structure)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'- an `fs.ReadStream`\n'[39m +
                    [32m'- the return value of our `toFile` helper\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```ts\n'[39m +
                    [32m"import fs from 'fs';\n"[39m +
                    [32m"import Inconvo, { toFile } from '@inconvoai/node';\n"[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const client = new Inconvo();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'// If you have access to Node `fs` we recommend using `fs.createReadStream()`:\n'[39m +
                    [32m"await client.agents.datasets.user.upload('user_123', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  agentId: 'agentId',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  file: fs.createReadStream('/path/to/file'),\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'// Or if you have the web `File` API you can pass a `File` instance:\n'[39m +
                    [32m"await client.agents.datasets.user.upload('user_123', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  agentId: 'agentId',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  file: new File(['my bytes'], 'file'),\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'// You can also pass a `fetch` `Response`:\n'[39m +
                    [32m"await client.agents.datasets.user.upload('user_123', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  agentId: 'agentId',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  file: await fetch('https://somesite/file'),\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'// Finally, if none of the above are convenient, you can use our `toFile` helper:\n'[39m +
                    [32m"await client.agents.datasets.user.upload('user_123', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  agentId: 'agentId',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  file: await toFile(Buffer.from('my bytes'), 'file'),\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m"await client.agents.datasets.user.upload('user_123', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  agentId: 'agentId',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  file: await toFile(new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2]), 'file'),\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'## Handling errors\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'When the library is unable to connect to the API,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'or if the API returns a non-success status code (i.e., 4xx or 5xx response),\n'[39m +
                    [32m'a subclass of `APIError` will be thrown:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'<!-- prettier-ignore -->\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```ts\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const conversation = await client.agents.conversations\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  .create('agentId', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"    userIdentifier: 'user_123',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"    userContext: { orgId: 'org_456' },\n"[39m +
                    [32m'  })\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  .catch(async (err) => {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    if (err instanceof Inconvo.APIError) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'      console.log(err.status); // 400\n'[39m +
                    [32m'      console.log(err.name); // BadRequestError\n'[39m +
                    [32m"      console.log(err.headers); // {server: 'nginx', ...}\n"[39m +
                    [32m'    } else {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'      throw err;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  });\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Error codes are as follows:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'| Status Code | Error Type                 |\n'[39m +
                    [32m'| ----------- | -------------------------- |\n'[39m +
                    [32m'| 400         | `BadRequestError`          |\n'[39m +
                    [32m'| 401         | `AuthenticationError`      |\n'[39m +
                    [32m'| 403         | `PermissionDeniedError`    |\n'[39m +
                    [32m'| 404         | `NotFoundError`            |\n'[39m +
                    [32m'| 422         | `UnprocessableEntityError` |\n'[39m +
                    [32m'| 429         | `RateLimitError`           |\n'[39m +
                    [32m'| >=500       | `InternalServerError`      |\n'[39m +
                    [32m'| N/A         | `APIConnectionError`       |\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'### Retries\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Certain errors will be automatically retried 2 times by default, with a short exponential backoff.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Connection errors (for example, due to a network connectivity problem), 408 Request Timeout, 409 Conflict,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'429 Rate Limit, and >=500 Internal errors will all be retried by default.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'You can use the `maxRetries` option to configure or disable this:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'<!-- prettier-ignore -->\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```js\n'[39m +
                    [32m'// Configure the default for all requests:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const client = new Inconvo({\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  maxRetries: 0, // default is 2\n'[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'// Or, configure per-request:\n'[39m +
                    [32m"await client.agents.conversations.create('agentId', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  userIdentifier: 'user_123',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  userContext: { orgId: 'org_456' },\n"[39m +
                    [32m'}, {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  maxRetries: 5,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'### Timeouts\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Requests time out after 1 minute by default. You can configure this with a `timeout` option:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'<!-- prettier-ignore -->\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```ts\n'[39m +
                    [32m'// Configure the default for all requests:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const client = new Inconvo({\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  timeout: 20 * 1000, // 20 seconds (default is 1 minute)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'// Override per-request:\n'[39m +
                    [32m"await client.agents.conversations.create('agentId', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  userIdentifier: 'user_123',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  userContext: { orgId: 'org_456' },\n"[39m +
                    [32m'}, {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  timeout: 5 * 1000,\n'[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'On timeout, an `APIConnectionTimeoutError` is thrown.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Note that requests which time out will be [retried twice by default](#retries).\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'## Auto-pagination\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'List methods in the Inconvo API are paginated.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'You can use the `for await … of` syntax to iterate through items across all pages:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```ts\n'[39m +
                    [32m'async function fetchAllConversationListResponses(params) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  const allConversationListResponses = [];\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  // Automatically fetches more pages as needed.\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  for await (const conversationListResponse of client.agents.conversations.list('agentId', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m'    limit: 20,\n'[39m +
                    [32m"    userIdentifier: 'user_123',\n"[39m +
                    [32m'  })) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'    allConversationListResponses.push(conversationListResponse);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  }\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  return allConversationListResponses;\n'[39m +
                    [32m'}\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Alternatively, you can request a single page at a time:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```ts\n'[39m +
                    [32m"let page = await client.agents.conversations.list('agentId', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m'  limit: 20,\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  userIdentifier: 'user_123',\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'for (const conversationListResponse of page.items) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  console.log(conversationListResponse);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'}\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'// Convenience methods are provided for manually paginating:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'while (page.hasNextPage()) {\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  page = await page.getNextPage();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  // ...\n'[39m +
                    [32m'}\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'## Advanced Usage\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'### Accessing raw Response data (e.g., headers)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'The "raw" `Response` returned by `fetch()` can be accessed through the `.asResponse()` method on the `APIPromise` type that all methods return.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'This method returns as soon as the headers for a successful response are received and does not consume the response body, so you are free to write custom parsing or streaming logic.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'You can also use the `.withResponse()` method to get the raw `Response` along with the parsed data.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Unlike `.asResponse()` this method consumes the body, returning once it is parsed.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'<!-- prettier-ignore -->\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```ts\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const client = new Inconvo();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const response = await client.agents.conversations\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  .create('agentId', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"    userIdentifier: 'user_123',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"    userContext: { orgId: 'org_456' },\n"[39m +
                    [32m'  })\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  .asResponse();\n'[39m +
                    [32m"console.log(response.headers.get('X-My-Header'));\n"[39m +
                    [32m'console.log(response.statusText); // access the underlying Response object\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const { data: conversation, response: raw } = await client.agents.conversations\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  .create('agentId', {\n"[39m +
                    [32m"    userIdentifier: 'user_123',\n"[39m +
                    [32m"    userContext: { orgId: 'org_456' },\n"[39m +
                    [32m'  })\n'[39m +
                    [32m'  .withResponse();\n'[39m +
                    [32m"console.log(raw.headers.get('X-My-Header'));\n"[39m +
                    [32m'console.log(conversation.id);\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'### Logging\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'> [!IMPORTANT]\n'[39m +
                    [32m'> All log messages are intended for debugging only. The format and content of log messages\n'[39m +
                    [32m'> may change between releases.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'#### Log levels\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'The log level can be configured in two ways:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'1. Via the `INCONVO_LOG` environment variable\n'[39m +
                    [32m'2. Using the `logLevel` client option (overrides the environment variable if set)\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```ts\n'[39m +
                    [32m"import Inconvo from '@inconvoai/node';\n"[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const client = new Inconvo({\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  logLevel: 'debug', // Show all log messages\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Available log levels, from most to least verbose:\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m"- `'debug'` - Show debug messages, info, warnings, and errors\n"[39m +
                    [32m"- `'info'` - Show info messages, warnings, and errors\n"[39m +
                    [32m"- `'warn'` - Show warnings and errors (default)\n"[39m +
                    [32m"- `'error'` - Show only errors\n"[39m +
                    [32m"- `'off'` - Disable all logging\n"[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m"At the `'debug'` level, all HTTP requests and responses are logged, including headers and bodies.\n"[39m +
                    [32m'Some authentication-related headers are redacted, but sensitive data in request and response bodies\n'[39m +
                    [32m'may still be visible.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'#### Custom logger\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'By default, this library logs to `globalThis.console`. You can also provide a custom logger.\n'[39m +
                    [32m"Most logging libraries are supported, including [pino](https://www.npmjs.com/package/pino), [winston](https://www.npmjs.com/package/winston), [bunyan](https://www.npmjs.com/package/bunyan), [consola](https://www.npmjs.com/package/consola), [signale](https://www.npmjs.com/package/signale), and [@std/log](https://jsr.io/@std/log). If your logger doesn't work, please open an issue.\n"[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'When providing a custom logger, the `logLevel` option still controls which messages are emitted, messages\n'[39m +
                    [32m'below the configured level will not be sent to your logger.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```ts\n'[39m +
                    [32m"import Inconvo from '@inconvoai/node';\n"[39m +
                    [32m"import pino from 'pino';\n"[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const logger = pino();\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'const client = new Inconvo({\n'[39m +
                    [32m"  logger: logger.child({ name: 'Inconvo' }),\n"[39m +
                    [32m"  logLevel: 'debug', // Send all messages to pino, allowing it to filter\n"[39m +
                    [32m'});\n'[39m +
                    [32m'```\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'### Making custom/undocumented requests\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'This library is typed for convenient access to the documented API. If you need to access undocumented\n'[39m +
                    [32m'endpoints, params, or response properties, the library can still be used.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'#### Undocumented endpoints\n'[39m +
                    [32m'\n'[39m +
                    [32m'To make requests to undocumented endpoints, you can use `client.get`, `client.post`, and other HTTP verbs.\n'[39m +
                    [32m'Options on the client, such as retries, will be respected when making these'[39m... 4838 more characters,
                  isTruncated: [33mfalse[39m
                }
              },
              providerOptions: {
                vertex: {
                  thoughtSignature: [32m''[39m
                }
              }
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          role: [32m'assistant'[39m,
          content: [
            {
              type: [32m'reasoning'[39m,

                [32m'\n'[39m +

            {

              output: {
                type: [32m'json'[39m,

                  matches: [
                    {
                      file: [32m'home/user/inconvo-app/node_modules/@inconvoai/node/src/internal/uploads.ts'[39m,

                      context: [32m'    messages: Array<InconvoConversation.Message>;'[39m

                    {

                      line: [33m49[39m,
                      context: [32m' * confusing error messages later on.'[39m
                    },
                    {
                      file: [32m'home/user/inconvo-app/node_modules/@inconvoai/node/README.md'[39m,
                      line: [33m260[39m,
                      context: [32m'> All log messages are intended for debugging only. The format and content of log messages'[39m
                    },
                    {
                      file: [32m'home/user/inconvo-app/node_modules/@inconvoai/node/README.md'[39m,
                      line: [33m274[39m,
                      context: [32m"  logLevel: 'debug', // Show all log messages"[39m
                    },
                    {
                      file: [32m'home/user/inconvo-app/node_modules/@inconvoai/node/README.md'[39m,
                      line: [33m280[39m,
                      context: [32m"- `'debug'` - Show debug messages, info, warnings, and errors"[39m
                    },
                    {
                      file: [32m'home/user/inconvo-app/node_modules/@inconvoai/node/README.md'[39m,
                      line: [33m281[39m,
                      context: [32m"- `'info'` - Show info messages, warnings, and errors"[39m

                      line: [33m58[39m,
                      context: [32m' * confusing error messages later on.'[39m
